What it does: Total width of the accessible interior in kilometers (square world assumed).
How the map is bounded with natural borders.
Border is a hazard belt plus a hard wall (recommended).
Smaller world with a short hazard belt and fast pacing.
Width of the hazard belt before the hard wall.
If true, there is a final non-traversable wall after the hazard belt.
World streaming tile size for terrain/biome/POI streaming buckets.
Resolution of the biome field sampling (macro assignment).
Width of biome blending zone for visuals and spawn interpolation.
Scale of the macro biome noise field (lower = larger patches).
Scale of the micro feature noise field (higher = finer features).
Minimum connected area in biome cells; smaller patches are merged into neighbors.

Biome tags a POI cannot spawn in.
Required habitat tags (e.g., Caves, AbandonedStructures) when applicable.
Relative spawn budget multiplier per biome (used as a per-biome scalar).
Relative flora density multiplier per biome (used as a per-biome scalar).
